title: The Creators of Claude Code and OpenClaw don't Prompt Their Agents Anymore?!
summary: Top AI engineers use self-prompting loops instead of manual prompting, where agents prompt agents via an orchestrator that spawns parallel workers. This scales agent capability but requires cost control and hallucination mitigation through observability dashboards.
writeup: Cole Medin explains loop-based orchestration, a pattern where AI engineers automate agent prompting instead of issuing prompts by hand. An orchestrator chains agents together and spawns parallel workers for tasks, storing observations in a database for monitoring and reliability. The trade-offs are direct: the method cuts manual prompting but raises token costs and can compound hallucinations without an observability harness in place.
